description: Hardcoded Cloudflare zone ID exposed in source code.
suggested_fix: Move the zone ID to an external configuration file or environment variable.

description: Missing validation for $app.stage; if undefined, domain becomes 'undefined.dev.opencode.ai'.
suggested_fix: Add a default case or throw an error when $app.stage is not one of the expected values.

description: Duplicated logic for generating domain and shortDomain.
suggested_fix: Extract a helper function that takes a base domain and returns the appropriate value based on $app.stage.

description: Unnecessary IIFE used to compute a constant value.
suggested_fix: Replace with a simple ternary expression or a dedicated function.

description: Tight coupling to global $app variable makes the file harder to test and reuse.
suggested_fix: Pass the stage value as a parameter or retrieve it from a configuration context.

description: Hardcoded secret values accessed via SECRET.R2AccessKey.value and SECRET.R2SecretKey.value. If the ./secret file contains actual secret values committed to the repository, this leads to secret exposure.
suggested_fix: Ensure secrets are managed via a secure secret store (e.g., AWS Secrets Manager, Cloudflare Secrets) and referenced via SST Secret constructs without committing raw values. If using SST secrets, define them as sst.Secret and use .value only in environment (which is safe), but verify ./secret does not contain plaintext secrets.

description: No validation that SECRET.R2AccessKey.value and SECRET.R2SecretKey.value are defined; missing or undefined secrets would cause runtime errors when the function starts.
suggested_fix: Add validation checks (e.g., if (!SECRET.R2AccessKey.value) throw new Error('Missing R2 access key')) before using the secrets, or rely on SST's built-in secret validation which will fail deployment if missing.

description: No validation that SECRET.R2SecretKey.value is defined; missing or undefined secrets would cause runtime errors when the function starts.
suggested_fix: Add validation checks for the secret key as well, or rely on SST's built-in secret validation.
